title: Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Jharkhand

overview.short: Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Indian state of Jharkhand.
overview.description: Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Indian state of Jharkhand. It was inaugurated in 1975 and provides habitat for an Indian elephant population.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Bhumij Revolt
topPlaces.description: The Bhumij Revolt (also known as Ganga Narayan's Hungama, Jungle Mahal Uprising) was revolt of Bhumij tribals, led by Ganga Narayan Singh during 1832–33 in the Manbhum and Jungle Mahal areas of the erstwhile Midnapore district, Bengal. The British have called it "Ganga Narain's Hangama" while historians have also written it as the Chuar rebellion.

topPlaces.name: Dimna Lake
topPlaces.description: Dimna Lake is an artificial reservoir located near Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary, 15 km from the city of Jamshedpur, in the state of Jharkhand, India. Constructed in 1944, by Tata Steel to deal with the water scarcity in the city.

topPlaces.name: Kopali
topPlaces.description: Kopali (also spelled as Kapali) is a census town in the Chandil CD block in the Chandil subdivision of the Seraikela Kharsawan district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.

topPlaces.name: Al Kabir Polytechnic
topPlaces.description: Al-Kabir Polytechnic, also known as Al Kabir Institute of Management and Technology, is a private institute in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, India. Established in 1990 by Abdul Hakim, it is one of the oldest private polytechnic institutes in eastern India.

topPlaces.name: Mango (Jamshedpur)
topPlaces.description: Mango is a suburb in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand. The city is situated across the Subarnarkeha River and is connected to Jamshedpur via three bridges laid side by side.

topPlaces.name: Boram block
topPlaces.description: Boram block is a CD block that forms an administrative division in the Dhalbhum subdivision of East Singhbhum district, in the Indian state of Jharkhand.

topPlaces.name: Boram, Purvi Singhbhum
topPlaces.description: Boram is a village in the Boram CD block in the Dhalbhum subdivision of the East Singhbhum district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.

topPlaces.name: Kharkai River
topPlaces.description: The Kharkai River is a river in eastern India. It is one of the major tributaries of the Subarnarekha River.

The British have called it "Ganga Narain's Hangama" while historians have also written it as the Chuar rebellion.

Dimna Lake is an artificial reservoir located near Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary, 15 km from the city of Jamshedpur, in the state of Jharkhand, India. Constructed in 1944, by Tata Steel to deal with the water scarcity in the city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (102 km) and the nearest railway station is Jamshedpur (Tatanagar) (13 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dalma Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bhumij Revolt, Dimna Lake, Kopali, Al Kabir Polytechnic, Mango (Jamshedpur).
